summary: Review #3 covers the greptile-apps[bot] review #2 fixes: (1) ESO kustomization wired into infrastructure/kustomization.yaml via `../../eso`, resolving the orphaned deploy/eso/ directory; (2) enable_pki() restructured with early return to prevent unconditional `bao secrets tune` on re-runs, fixing the idempotency contract; (3) documentation updated to describe `bao status -format=json` + jq JSON parsing instead of the stale exit-code approach. Three new/updated review patterns captured.

tests_checklist.item: Shell scripts pass shellcheck (tooling unavailable in this environment but code reviewed manually)
YAML manifests are syntactically valid (validated via Python yaml.safe_load)

code_quality_checklist.item: enable_pki early-return matches enable_kv_v2 pattern for consistency
Review patterns are well-structured with clear detection hints
No dead code or commented-out code introduced

security_checklist.item: No new user input handling surfaces
Kustomization wiring does not expose sensitive resources to unintended namespaces

architecture_checklist.item: ESO resources correctly placed in infrastructure kustomization (CRD-dependent, two-phase pattern)
Relative path ../../eso from deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/ resolves to deploy/eso/
Changes are minimal and scoped to external reviewer feedback

fail_fast_checklist.item: enable_pki returns early on existing engine — prevents unnecessary API calls
set -euo pipefail preserved in modified script

defensive_checklist.item: Idempotency guard in enable_pki now covers both enable and tune operations
Documentation idempotency section correctly describes JSON parsing guard
